entries.definition:
s–m–m poison, venom; pore of the body, very small hole, eye of a needle; hot, dust-carrying wind; people close to the self. Of this root, two forms occur four times in the Qurʾan: سمّ samm once and سموم samūm three times.
سمّ samm [n.] a very small hole *(7:40) سَمِّ ٱلْخِيَاطِ the eye of a needle.

السّموم ʾal-samūm [n.] scorching wind (also said to be cold wind) (15:27) وَٱلْجَآنَّ خَلَقْنَـٰهُ مِن قَبْلُ مِن نَّارِ ٱلسَّمُومِ the jinn We created before, from the fire of scorching wind.

سمّ samm [n.] a very small hole *(7:40) سَمِّ ٱلْخِيَاطِ the eye of a needle.

السّموم ʾal-samūm [n.] scorching wind (also said to be cold wind) (15:27) وَٱلْجَآنَّ خَلَقْنَـٰهُ مِن قَبْلُ مِن نَّارِ ٱلسَّمُومِ the jinn We created before, from the fire of scorching wind.
